hawkish_phrases: President Rosengren dissented because he judged that monetary policy was already accommodative and that additional accommodation was not needed
additional accommodation posed risks of further inflating the prices of risky assets and encouraging households and firms to take on too much leverage
President George dissented at this meeting because she believed that an unchanged setting of monetary policy was appropriate
business fixed investment and exports remained weak
Some participants favored maintaining the existing target range for the federal funds rate at this meeting
dovish_phrases: lower the target range for the federal funds rate to 1-1/2 to 1-3/4 percent
helping offset the effects on aggregate demand of weak global growth and trade developments, insuring against downside risks
promoting a more rapid return of inflation to the Committee's symmetric 2 percent objective
inflation continued to run below the Committee's symmetric 2 percent objective, and inflationary pressures remained muted
persistent inflation shortfalls could lead to a decline in longer-run inflation expectations
summary: The Committee voted 8-2 to cut the federal funds rate by 25 basis points to 1-1/2 to 1-3/4 percent, with two hawkish dissents from George and Rosengren, citing global developments, muted inflation pressures, and the need for insurance against downside risks, while removing forward guidance language to signal a likely pause.
